filename: Protocol for long term storage of sectioned slidse by paraffin dipping_13082024.docx
relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Spatial protocols/Protocol for long term storage of sectioned slidse by paraffin dipping_13082024.docx
full_text:
Protocol for coating sectioned tissues in slides with Paraffin wax coating.

Materials Needed:

Microscope slides with mounted specimens.

Paraffin wax Cat:00402-1

Hot air Oven

Plastic rack for slide holding

Absorbent paper

Gloves

Fume hood or well-ventilated area.

Beaker

Aluminium foil

Forceps

Protocol:

Prepare the Workspace:

Work in a well-ventilated area or under a fume hood.

Ensure that all slides are clean and dry before beginning the process.

Heat the paraffin wax in the oven o/n at 60 °C in a 1 litre Beaker covered with foil.

Coating the Slides:

Hold the slides from the label side using a forcep.

Dip the slide in the wax for a few seconds

Slowly remove the slide from the wax, allowing any excess wax to drip back into the container.

Immediately place the coated slide on absorbent paper (eg: baking paper) to cool and harden.

Inspecting the Coating:

Once the wax has hardened (this should only take a few minutes), inspect the slide to ensure it has a uniform coating. There should be no air bubbles or gaps in the wax.

If any imperfections are noticed, you can gently reheat the slide over the hot plate to melt the wax and allow it to reflow, or you may dip the slide again.

Storage:

Store the wax-coated slides in a dry, cool place. They should be kept in a slide box or another protective container to prevent physical damage.

Notes:

Temperature Control: Be mindful of the temperature when melting the wax; too high a temperature might damage the specimen or cause the wax to become too thin.

Safety Considerations:

Always wear gloves and use forceps to handle the slides while dipping them in hot wax.

Ensure proper ventilation to avoid inhaling any fumes from the hot wax.
